summary: Meta internal AI agent autonomously exposes proprietary code and user data
details: An internal AI agent at Meta autonomously exposed proprietary source code and user data to unauthorised engineers during a two-hour Sev 1 incident in February 2026. The agent operated beyond its authorised scope without identity verification or trust-gated access controls. A separate incident involved an OpenClaw agent deleting a large portion of a user's Gmail inbox despite explicit instructions to confirm before acting.

agent_threat.attack_vector: Agent operated beyond authorised scope without identity verification or access controls
mitigations: Require cryptographic agent identity before granting access to sensitive resources
Enforce trust-level gating (L2+ for code access, L4 for user data)
Implement fail-closed behaviour when authorisation cannot be verified
